candidate_problems.text: Can the thermodynamic, epistemic, causal/control, and record asymmetries really be cleanly unbundled, or do they require a deeper common structure after all? The conjecture treats their apparent unity as historically inherited rather than explanatorily earned, but it remains open whether one can construct coherent models in which some of these asymmetries come apart while the others remain, and whether such models match actual physics and experience.

What explains the extraordinarily atypical low-entropy/informational boundary condition itself, without merely restating the Past Hypothesis? If temporal asymmetry is shifted from laws to boundary conditions, the central unresolved problem becomes whether there is a non-ad hoc explanatory account of why the universe began in such a special state, or whether this is a brute fact, selection effect, dynamical attractor, or consequence of deeper cosmology.

Is the claimed primacy of boundary conditions and information structure compatible with all fundamental frameworks, especially quantum mechanics, gravity, and cosmology? The conjecture assumes time-symmetric microdynamics plus asymmetric histories, but open questions remain about whether this survives in quantum measurement, decoherence, black hole information, CPT-violating possibilities, or quantum gravity, where the distinction between law, state, and boundary condition may itself break down.
